# Nautilus

> Policy-first data broker for AI agents — one call plans, routes, enforces, attests, and audits every data access, so agents never touch data directly. Built on Fathom.

Nautilus brokers every data access behind a single request: it plans the query, routes to the right source, enforces policy (HIPAA / NIST / scope) via Fathom rules, attests the decision with a signed token, and writes an audit record. For regulated, air-gapped, and cleared workloads.

## Documentation
- [Home](https://nautilus.krakn.ai/latest/): What Nautilus is and the one-call broker model.
- [Getting Started](https://nautilus.krakn.ai/latest/getting-started/): Install, configure a source, run your first brokered request.

## Concepts
- [Architecture](https://nautilus.krakn.ai/latest/concepts/architecture/): Plan → route → enforce → attest → audit.
- [The Attestation Chain](https://nautilus.krakn.ai/latest/concepts/attestation-chain/): How signed tokens make each access provable.
- [RKM Rule Lifecycle](https://nautilus.krakn.ai/latest/concepts/rkm-lifecycle/): How Fathom rules govern routing decisions.

## How-to
- [Operator Guide](https://nautilus.krakn.ai/latest/how-to/operator-guide/): Deploy and run the broker.
- [Write a Routing Rule](https://nautilus.krakn.ai/latest/how-to/write-a-routing-rule/): Author policy that routes and scopes access.
- [Configure Attestation](https://nautilus.krakn.ai/latest/how-to/configure-attestation/): Sign and verify access tokens.
- [Add a Postgres Source](https://nautilus.krakn.ai/latest/how-to/add-a-postgres-source/): Connect a data source through an adapter.

## Reference
- [Python SDK](https://nautilus.krakn.ai/latest/reference/python-sdk/)
- [REST API](https://nautilus.krakn.ai/latest/reference/rest-api/)
- [CLI](https://nautilus.krakn.ai/latest/reference/cli/)
- [Rule Packs](https://nautilus.krakn.ai/latest/reference/rule-packs/)

## Links
- [PyPI: nautilus-rkm](https://pypi.org/project/nautilus-rkm/)
- [Source](https://github.com/KrakenNet/nautilus)
- [Fathom — the reasoning engine Nautilus is built on](https://github.com/KrakenNet/fathom)
